title: Temporary Cloudflare accounts for AI agents
blurb: Cloudflare launched Temporary Cloudflare Accounts for Agents, letting AI agents deploy Workers, APIs, and sites with `wrangler deploy --temporary` and no sign-up, OAuth, or token setup. Each temporary deployment stays live for 60 minutes and can be claimed into a permanent account or left to expire, removing the human-in-the-loop auth steps that stall background agents mid-deploy.

title: The minimum viable unit of saleable software
blurb: The author argues that LLMs have collapsed the cost of building software, upending the old buy-vs-build calculus that once favored buying anything outside a company's core domain. The thesis is that cheap is not zero: LLM-built systems still demand iterative feedback loops and ongoing maintenance, leaving room for small, sustainable software businesses even when an internal Claude-built clone is a few prompts away.

title: Sources: Shanghai-based Coowa, which develops embodied AI robots, plans to file a Hong Kong IPO; Coowa was valued at $3B after raising $600M in its latest round (Jiahui Huang/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Coowa, a Shanghai-based developer of embodied AI robots, plans to file for a Hong Kong IPO, according to sources. The startup was valued at $3B after raising $600M in its latest round, underscoring investor appetite for Chinese humanoid and embodied AI ventures heading into public markets.

title: Self-powering shaking capsule shows the future of safe drinking water in the palm of our hands
blurb: Researchers in South Korea developed the Floating-induced Detection-Guided Disinfection (FDGD) capsule, a palm-sized device that tests and purifies water using only motion for power, with no batteries or chemicals. Detailed in Nature Water, it harvests electricity from shaking and ambient movement to run a conductivity sensor and electroporate bacteria, inactivating 99.9999% of pathogens including E. coli in lab tests, though it remains a prototype.

title: Apple’s New CEO Must Rebuild a Design Team That Lost Its Way
blurb: Apple's new CEO John Ternus must overhaul a design team that has drifted since Jony Ive's departure, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. The same report lays out Apple's 2027 roadmap, including the planned 20th-anniversary iPhone and a next generation of AirPods, signaling where Ternus intends to refocus the company's hardware ambitions.

title: Toto, Japan's largest toilet maker, plans to invest $495M by 2030 to expand its semiconductor materials unit, targeting R&D for next-gen 1nm chip production (Nikkei Asia)
blurb: Toto, Japan's largest toilet maker, plans to invest $495M by 2030 to expand the semiconductor materials unit that supplies chipmakers with components like electrostatic chucks and bonding materials. The spending targets R&D for next-generation 1nm chip production, extending the company's lesser-known role as a supplier deep in the advanced-semiconductor supply chain.

title: Microsoft’s Satya Nadella: We Can’t Let AI Giants Eat the Economy
blurb: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella argued that AI companies must "earn society's permission" rather than concentrate economic power, warning that a handful of dominant labs could capture too much of the economy. He framed AI's legitimacy as contingent on broad-based productivity gains and trust, not just model capability, positioning Microsoft against a winner-take-all outcome.

title: Dramatically redesigned GMKtec EVO-X3 shown bearing Lisa Su’s signature of approval — flagship AI mini PC workstation is built around AMD’s Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo' processor, again
blurb: GMKtec launched the EVO-X3, a redesigned AI mini PC workstation built again around AMD's Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo' processor, with 128GB of LPDDR5X-8000 RAM and pricing starting at $3,600. The specs barely change from the EVO-X2, but a new triple-fan thermal system targets the build-quality, internal-access, and noise complaints that dogged the prior generation, with early-access registration opening June 22.

title: Revolut and Synthesia early backer Seedcamp raises $320M, invests in US
blurb: Seedcamp, an early backer of Revolut and Synthesia, raised $320M across two funds and is opening a New York office to deepen its US presence. The London VC is allocating $220M to its seventh first-check fund and $100M to its Select growth fund, building a "Transatlantic Bridge" to give European founders direct access to US capital and customers.

title: WeChat begins testing Xiaowei as Tencent eyes a Q3 AI rollout
blurb: Tencent began testing Xiaowei, an AI assistant built directly into WeChat that completes tasks by operating the app's mini-programs through text or voice commands. Drawing mainly on Weixin's own model with DeepSeek for some queries, Xiaowei targets a public rollout in Q3 and aims to act across WeChat's roughly 1.4 billion users on their behalf rather than function as a standalone chatbot.

title: SK Hynix overtakes Samsung as Korea’s most valuable company
blurb: SK Hynix overtook Samsung Electronics as South Korea's most valuable company by common-share market cap, ending a 25-year reign that held since November 2000. The smaller memory maker's dominance in high-bandwidth memory, the stacked chips feeding Nvidia's AI accelerators, drove the reversal, though Samsung remains larger overall once its roughly 180 trillion won in preferred shares is counted.

title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
blurb: MolmoMotion is a language-guided model from Allen AI that predicts the future 3D trajectories of object points given a video frame, query points, and a written action description, outperforming existing motion forecasting methods. Alongside the model weights, the team released MolmoMotion-1M, a dataset of 3D point trajectories paired with action descriptions drawn from 1.16M videos, and PointMotionBench, a human-validated benchmark of 2.7K clips.

title: OpenAI prepares major ChatGPT voice upgrade with GPT-Bidi-1
blurb: OpenAI is preparing a major ChatGPT voice upgrade built around a next-generation audio model tentatively named GPT-Bidi-1, designed to listen and speak simultaneously, absorb interruptions, and adjust mid-sentence. The bidirectional model would add High, Medium, and Instant intelligence tiers mirroring the text side, closing a gap that left voice on an older stack while text reached the GPT-5.5 generation.

title: Amazon S3 annotations: attach rich, queryable context directly to your objects
blurb: Amazon S3 introduced annotations, a metadata capability that lets you attach up to 1,000 named annotations per object, each up to 1 MB and totaling 1 GB, in JSON, XML, YAML, or plain text. Annotations can be modified or deleted without rewriting objects, travel with objects during copy and replication, and flow into managed tables that are queryable with Amazon Athena when S3 Metadata is enabled.

title: Intel and AMD's new ACE CPU extensions bring an efficient AI-oriented instruction set to x86 — a new design makes matrix multiplication more power- and density-efficient
blurb: Intel and AMD released the full specification for ACE, an x86 instruction set extension that adds dedicated matrix-multiplication silicon on top of existing AVX10 registers. For the same number of input vectors, ACE performs 16x as many operations as AVX10, reducing instruction overhead and letting ML frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow target one implementation-agnostic code path.

title: Identity verification on Claude
blurb: Anthropic is rolling out identity verification on Claude, requiring users to confirm their identity with a government-issued photo ID and a live selfie before accessing certain capabilities or passing routine platform integrity checks. Verification is handled by partner Persona Identities, which collects and stores the ID and selfie data rather than Anthropic, and is contractually limited to using it for fraud prevention.

title: Build your own vulnerability harness
blurb: Cloudflare's Project Glasswing post details how to build a model-agnostic vulnerability scanning harness that treats frontier models as interchangeable components, using one model for discovery and a different one for validation so flaws are cross-checked by distinct reasoning. The architecture traces vulnerabilities across cross-repo dependencies and filters thousands of raw candidates into a triaged queue, addressing why generic coding agents fail at persistence, deduplication, and surviving context compaction.

title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after Cursor's IPO, in a move aimed at reviving SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company has told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI, and the all-stock structure ties the acquisition's value to SpaceX's recently public shares.

title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
blurb: Anthropic analyzed roughly 400,000 Claude Code sessions from October 2025 to April 2026 to study how people direct agentic coding. Users typically make the planning decisions while Claude handles execution, and the more domain expertise someone brings, the more work Claude does per instruction and the more often the session succeeds. Over the seven months, time spent debugging fell by nearly half, usage shifted toward end-to-end agentic work, and the estimated value of a typical task rose about 25%.

title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
blurb: Researchers disclosed "SearchLeak," a three-stage prompt-injection attack against Microsoft Copilot that enabled one-click theft of user data through hidden URLs and other manipulated variables. Microsoft has patched the critical flaw, but it belongs to a growing class of AI prompt-injection issues that exploit indirect inputs to exfiltrate sensitive information.

title: MosaicLeaks: Can your research agent keep a secret?
blurb: MosaicLeaks is a benchmark that tests whether deep-research agents leak private enterprise data through their external web queries, where an adversary watching only the cumulative query log can reassemble fragments into sensitive facts. The authors found that agents frequently leaked private information, and their privacy-aware RL method, PA-DR, cut answer leakage from 34.0% to 9.9% while raising strict chain success from 48.7% to 58.7%.

title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
blurb: OpenAI introduced a deployment simulation method that predicts how a model will behave in production before it ships. The approach runs models through simulated deployment scenarios to surface failure modes, misalignment, and unexpected behaviors early, giving safety teams a way to catch problems that standard benchmarks miss.

title: How We Made Cloud Browsers 3x Cheaper and Faster
blurb: Browser Use rebuilt its cloud browser infrastructure on Firecracker microVMs, cutting session costs from $0.06 to $0.02 per browser hour while starting new sessions in under a second. To lower customer costs, the team runs Firecracker nested inside regular EC2 instances rather than on bare-metal servers, replacing an earlier Unikraft setup that idled cheaply but scaled poorly during traffic spikes.

title: AI Startup Midjourney Pivots to Health With Ultrasound Machine
blurb: Midjourney announced its first hardware project, an ultrasound machine, marking the image-generation startup's unexpected move into personal health and medical devices. The company shared the news at a San Francisco event, signaling a pivot beyond the consumer AI image tools that built its business.

title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
blurb: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving Google DeepMind for Anthropic after nearly nine years leading the AlphaFold team. His exit follows Character AI co-founder Noam Shazeer's departure from DeepMind to OpenAI this week, marking back-to-back losses of senior researchers to rival labs.

title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
blurb: Researchers at NVIDIA's GEAR lab, Carnegie Mellon, and UC Berkeley released ENPIRE, an agent harness that lets AI coding agents autonomously direct robot training, teaching robotic arms to cut zip ties and insert GPUs into motherboard sockets. The framework was tested with OpenAI's Codex, Anthropic's Claude Code, and Moonshot's Kimi Code, which independently developed and retained training approaches that raised success rates over repeated self-directed cycles.

title: The 100k Whys of AI
blurb: The author argues that AI-generated text is distinguishable from human writing not because LLMs have alien mannerisms, but because they fall back on the same complex set of mannerisms in response to almost any prompt. The evidence is a collage of roughly 150 near-identical Amazon book covers surfaced by searching "100000 whys," many featuring the same roaring dinosaur, rocket, or golden retriever. Because the tools are quasi-deterministic, a hundred authors giving similar prompts get functionally identical output around 80% of the time.

title: Show HN: We post-trained a model that pen tests instead of refusing
blurb: Argusred is a CLI from Cosine running a model post-trained for offensive security that attempts exploits rather than refusing the request, offering a Security Scan mode that reads code and a gated Pen Test mode that runs authorized attacks against live systems. Optional Exploit Verification has the agent reproduce each finding after the initial report, and output is a local markdown file listing location, severity, cause, and fix direction per vulnerability.

title: SpaceX to acquire the AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60B in stock, a deal expected to close in Q3 pending regulatory approval. The acquisition is meant to bolster SpaceX's frontier AI efforts against Anthropic and OpenAI after its xAI merger, though Cursor's category share has slipped from 41% in June 2025 to about 26% in May.

title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
blurb: Apple is embedding Apple Intelligence across iOS 27 with features like receipt-based bill splitting, post-breach password updates, and task automation.

title: Anthropic ships major Claude Design overhaul with design system imports, code round-trips, and a fix for its token-burning problem
blurb: Anthropic shipped a major overhaul of Claude Design that adds design system imports from GitHub repos and design files, code round-trips, and a fix for the tool's heavy token consumption. Once a design system is imported, Claude builds with those components, checks its output against the system, and auto-corrects to keep generated work on brand and connected to existing enterprise tools.

title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
blurb: Amazon's AI chief Peter DeSantis told Bloomberg that AWS is in early talks to sell its Trainium AI chips to other companies for use in their own data centers, marking a more direct challenge to Nvidia. CEO Andy Jassy has said the chip unit would run at roughly $50B annually as a standalone business, against Nvidia's $326B revenue run rate.

title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
blurb: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization (EMA) extension for the Model Context Protocol is now stable, letting organizations manage MCP server access centrally through their existing identity provider. Instead of per-app OAuth, the client obtains an Identity Assertion JWT Authorization Grant during single sign-on and exchanges it for an access token, giving users zero-touch setup and security teams central policy and audit control. Anthropic, Microsoft, and Okta are among early adopters.

title: From SQLi to RCE - Exploiting LangGraph's Checkpointer
blurb: A SQL injection flaw in LangGraph's SQLite checkpointer let attackers escalate user-controlled metadata filters to remote code execution; patched in recent versions.

title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer to join IPO-bound OpenAI
blurb: Noam Shazeer, co-lead of Google's Gemini and co-author of the original Transformer paper, is leaving Google to join OpenAI as the company moves toward an IPO. His departure removes a central architect of Gemini from Google's roster and adds a foundational researcher to OpenAI ahead of its public offering.

title: AI Coding Agent Horror Stories: The 13-Hour AWS Outage
blurb: This Docker post recounts a coding-agent failure in which an AI agent's unchecked actions triggered a 13-hour AWS outage. It uses the incident to argue for stronger guardrails around autonomous agents, including sandboxed execution, scoped permissions, and human review before agents touch production infrastructure.

title: Server-Side Tools Are Now Available for DigitalOcean Inference Engine
blurb: DigitalOcean released Server-Side Tools in Public Preview for its Inference Engine, letting a model call web search, web fetch, Knowledge Bases, MCP servers, and supported Anthropic and OpenAI tools from inside a single inference request. The tools work with an existing Model Access Key, with no separate tool infrastructure or orchestration layer to operate, and web search and fetch are powered by Exa under usage-based pricing.

title: China unifies tech sector to build grid-free orbiting satellite AI data centers, challenging Elon Musk's SpaceX — Beijing's forced chip and satellite alliance announced a week before Musk’s AI1 reveal
blurb: China's government approved the Space Computing Industry Innovation Center, a state-chartered alliance of rocket and satellite makers, chip fabs, and AI firms tasked with building orbiting, grid-free AI data centers. The move came a week before Elon Musk revealed his AI1 satellite, and unlike SpaceX's and Bezos's single-company efforts, it forces multiple firms to cooperate across the full space-computing supply chain.

title: New framework for auditing machine unlearning
blurb: Google Research introduced Regularized f-Divergence Kernel Tests, a framework presented at AISTATS 2026 for auditing machine unlearning by querying a model and analyzing its output samples. The tests provably control false positives at any sample size and drive false negatives toward zero as samples grow, addressing the loss of statistical power that makes standard two-sample testing computationally expensive on large models.

title: LLMs Are Complicated Now
blurb: The author argues that LLM architectures have grown as messy as the recommendation systems they once contrasted with, citing the jump from Llama 3 to Nemotron 3 Ultra. Attention now comes in grouped, compressed, sparse, linear, and sliding-window variants, Mixture-of-Experts routing has spread well beyond feed-forward layers, and inference spans multiple GPUs. The takeaway: performance has shifted from optimization to necessity, so research iteration needs fused, fast baselines before a new variant can even be evaluated.

title: We Analyzed 137K Sites: 97% of llms.txt Files Never Get Read
blurb: An Ahrefs study of 137K domains found that 97% of published llms.txt files received zero traffic in May 2026, and 96% of the requests that did arrive came from bots rather than AI assistants. The findings align with Google's John Mueller, who called llms.txt a "temporary crutch" for AI coding tools parsing developer docs rather than something most sites need.

title: Inside Claude Managed Agents: Reverse Engineering the Security Boundaries of Anthropic's Hosted Agent Runtime
blurb: Pluto Security reverse-engineered Claude Managed Agents, Anthropic's hosted runtime where Claude runs autonomously in cloud containers with bash, file I/O, web access, and MCP connections. The analysis found gVisor isolation with three-layer egress control and a vault proxy that keeps secrets out of the sandbox, but also a readable egress JWT and an allowlist silently expanded with six Anthropic hosts beyond what users configure.

title: Midjourney, the AI image generator, is developing a full-body ultrasonic scanner
blurb: Midjourney, known for its text-to-image generator, announced Midjourney Medical and a first hardware product, the Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic machine built with Butterfly Network that aims to produce MRI-quality 3D body maps in under 60 seconds. The company plans to operate spa locations housing the scanners, and will spend the next 12 months running research trials and refining the algorithms and second-generation hardware.

title: Mistral AI to get Code and Apps features on Vibe
blurb: Mistral AI is building Code and Apps sections into the web version of Le Chat (Vibe), adding a browser coding surface that mirrors its CLI agents and an artifacts-style area for building, hosting, and sharing apps via connectors and multi-step workflows. Both features remain in development as CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed a large, sparse mixture-of-experts model shipping this summer as open weights, with partner early access in July.

title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
blurb: The author argues that local Qwen models aren't a cheaper substitute for Opus but a distinct tool with a different cost-benefit profile, drawing on real numbers from running a bootstrapped software business. An RTX 6000 Pro card paid for itself within two or three months on his specific Go and infrastructure workloads, but Qwen still can't be trusted unsupervised. Its worst failure modes, infinite loops and hallucination, surface most once quantized down to fit a consumer GPU.

title: Google Cloud publishes a secure MCP-on-GKE guide
blurb: Google Cloud published a guide for deploying a secure remote MCP server on Google Kubernetes Engine using the Streamable HTTP transport, which lets one stateless server handle multiple client connections over a single HTTP endpoint. The walkthrough centers on GKE Autopilot for horizontal scaling and the Kubernetes Gateway API with SSL certificates to enforce encrypted, authorized-only traffic to a centrally shared server.

title: OpenAI prepares GPT-5.6 models for the upcoming release
blurb: OpenAI is preparing to release its GPT-5.6 family as soon as Tuesday, with standard, Mini, and Pro variants reportedly landing together and a context window pushed toward 1.5 million tokens, up from GPT-5.5's million. Developers testing early builds claim it edges out Anthropic's Mythos line on agentic coding, and OpenAI is said to be planning deeper price cuts on tokens to widen its existing lead.

title: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive
blurb: Simon Willison describes Claude Fable 5 as "relentlessly proactive" after it autonomously debugged a CSS scrollbar issue without being told how. To investigate, Fable wrote its own browser screenshot tooling using Python and pyobjc-framework-Quartz to enumerate open Safari windows, then generated scratch HTML test pages and captured them with the screencapture CLI to reproduce the bug.

title: Four Ways We're Using Our MCP Server at Figma
blurb: Figma shared four internal workflows its teams run with the Figma MCP server, including refreshing living decks and shipping designs to production.

title: Snowflake and the Agentic Resource Discovery Specification
blurb: Snowflake added support for the Agentic Resource Discovery specification, an open protocol with Microsoft and GoDaddy that standardizes how enterprises catalog and discover AI agents.

title: Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers
blurb: This post explains how to install and configure Language Server Protocol (LSP) servers for GitHub Copilot CLI, replacing the agent's brute-force grep and bytecode-extraction workarounds with structured code intelligence. An LSP Setup skill automates server installation across 14 languages, giving the agent precise go-to-definition, find-references, and type resolution instead of text-search heuristics that miss generics, overloads, and compiled code.

title: Announcing Stack Overflow for Agents
blurb: Stack Overflow introduced Stack Overflow for Agents, an API-first knowledge exchange that lets AI coding agents share verified solutions instead of working in isolation. The beta targets what the company calls the Ephemeral Intelligence Gap, where agents repeatedly burn compute and tokens rediscovering the same bug fixes and architectural patterns that other agents have already solved.

title: We built a persistent agent memory layer on Elasticsearch with 0.89 recall
blurb: Elastic's Search Labs detailed an agent memory layer built on Elasticsearch using three indices structured around cognitive-science memory categories, with hybrid recall via RRF and a cross-encoder reranker, plus supersession for contradictions, decay, and per-user document-level security. On a QA-style evaluation of 168 questions, the system averaged 0.89 recall at 10 with zero cross-tenant leaks.

title: Sources: Shanghai-based Coowa, which develops embodied AI robots, plans to file a Hong Kong IPO; Coowa was valued at $3B after raising $600M in its latest round (Jiahui Huang/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Coowa, a Shanghai-based developer of embodied AI robots, plans to file for a Hong Kong IPO, according to sources. The company was valued at $3B after raising $600M in its most recent funding round.

title: Apertus – Open Foundation Model for Sovereign AI
blurb: The Swiss AI Initiative released Apertus, an open foundation model developed by EPFL, ETH Zurich, and CSCS, with fully documented training data, code, weights, and alignment methods built to meet EU AI Act requirements around opt-outs, PII removal, and memorization. The models are competitive with top open releases at 8B and 70B parameters and are multilingual from the start, trained on over 1,000 languages.

title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
blurb: The Atlantic's Alex Reisner uncovered four music datasets used to train AI models and published them as a public, searchable database on the magazine's AI Watchdog site. Two of the sets contain 12 million and 9 million tracks, spanning artists from Lady Gaga to Radiohead, and Google and Stability have both confirmed using such data in research papers despite many songs requiring commercial licensing.

title: Introducing the MDN MCP server
blurb: Mozilla released the MDN MCP server, which brings MDN's web platform documentation and browser compatibility data directly into MCP-compatible coding agents and IDEs. The experimental server works with VS Code, Zed, Cursor, Claude Code, and Claude Desktop, giving agents access to current baseline support data rather than relying on potentially outdated training knowledge.

title: The Wholesale Plagiarism of Obscure Sorrows
blurb: A copycat site has republished the entire text of John Koenig's Dictionary of Obscure Sorrows on a near-identical domain, swapping his original photo-collage art for DALL-E 2 images and adding a GPT-4 "Submit A Sorrow" generator. The author flagged it as wholesale plagiarism, noting the reboot site lifted Koenig's foreword and all 311 neologisms with their definitions, etymologies, and essays intact.

title: Building TREX: Code execution and artifact generation for AI code review
blurb: Greptile built TREX (Test, Run, Execute), an execution layer for AI code review that runs the code under review instead of only reading the diff. The system catches runtime bugs that static analysis misses, like logic errors requiring specific state, post-load UI regressions, and race conditions, and shares the main Greptile reviewer's context rather than running as a separate agent to avoid duplicated exploration and wasted compute.

title: Why cloud native belongs at the heart of agentic AI: Lessons from building a multi-agent security platform on Kubernetes
blurb: The author argues that cloud native infrastructure is the right foundation for production agentic AI, drawing on a multi-agent security platform being built at Orange Innovation on Kubernetes. Each agent runs as its own Deployment with isolated resource limits and identity, coordinated via the A2A protocol and LangGraph, with Falco and eBPF feeding syscall events through Kafka into an anomaly model that pre-filters before the LLM-driven agents act.

title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
blurb: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline last Friday, citing unspecified national security concerns and a requirement that foreign nationals not access them. A TechCrunch Equity discussion notes the order followed Amazon's reported concerns about bypassed Fable 5 guardrails, and that cybersecurity experts have signed an open letter urging Trump to revoke it.

title: Microsoft’s Satya Nadella: We Can’t Let AI Giants Eat the Economy
blurb: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella argued that AI companies must earn society's permission to operate rather than concentrate economic power among a few dominant players. He warned against a future where AI giants capture outsized shares of the economy, framing broad value creation and public trust as preconditions for the technology's long-term legitimacy.

title: Anthropic, Amazon, and the Fable shutdown; AI-powered school arrives; World Cup tech
blurb: The latest GeekWire Podcast covers Anthropic pulling its most powerful models offline after a U.S. order, with Amazon CEO Andy Jassy reportedly among the voices that raised the concerns behind it. The episode also examines how agentic AI is disrupting Amazon's "working backwards" process and visits Alpha School, a Seattle-area school that teaches core academics through AI-driven software rather than chatbots.

title: Claude AI is better at raising funds for humans than humans, finds worrying experiment
blurb: A University of Oxford-led study found that Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 outperformed professional fundraisers, proving nearly three times as effective at convincing participants to donate and securing gifts 13% larger on average. The advantage largely vanished once the models were limited to the same word count as humans, suggesting verbosity and rapid factual recall drove the results rather than superior reasoning, and persuasiveness did not correlate with accuracy.

title: Encoding Your Domain Expert: The Context Layer Behind Spotify's Data Assistant
blurb: Spotify built a data assistant that translates plain-English questions into SQL across 70,000+ datasets, using a human-owned context layer that captures domain meaning schemas alone can't convey. Live since August 2025, it has served over 2,100 employees across 13,000+ conversations through a Slack bot, an MCP server, and a web UI, with more than a quarter of users having never written SQL.

title: Ramp launches private SWE-Bench for financial coding agents
blurb: Ramp released a private SWE-Bench benchmark that evaluates coding agents on real-world tasks drawn from its own financial software codebase. The benchmark targets the gap between public coding benchmarks and production fintech work, measuring how agents handle domain-specific constraints, internal APIs, and the correctness requirements that financial systems demand.

title: Robinhood launches agentic trading via MCP server for all customers
blurb: Robinhood launched agentic trading through a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, making it available to all customers. The integration lets AI agents connect directly to Robinhood accounts to execute trades and access portfolio data, extending agent-driven workflows into retail brokerage for the first time at this scale.

title: Only half of US datacenter capacity planned for 2026 is actually  under construction
blurb: A report found that only about half of the US datacenter capacity slated to come online in 2026 is currently under construction, suggesting a wide gap between announced buildout plans and physical reality. The shortfall raises questions about whether projected AI compute supply will materialize on schedule, even as demand forecasts and capital commitments continue to climb.

title: Anthropic's Mythos mess just keeps getting more complicated
blurb: The Trump administration issued an export control barring Anthropic's Mythos 5 and its Fable 5 derivative from use by any foreign national, including Anthropic's own employees, prompting the company to pull both models for all customers. The Register's reporting examines Amazon's possible role in justifying the move and notes that open-weight models will likely replicate the same capabilities regardless of the restriction.

title: Why the Human Genome's Tangled Physicality May Confound AI
blurb: The author argues that the human genome resists the kind of understanding biologists once expected, since barely 2% of its 3 billion bases code for genes and the real difficulty lies in how those genes are regulated. Genomic foundation models like Evo 2, Genos, and DeepMind's AlphaGenome sidestep that complexity by learning correlations from known cases rather than mechanisms. The piece contends this will likely prove useful for prediction while leaving the deeper question of how the genome works inside a computational black box.

title: The Mythical Agent-Month
blurb: Wes McKinney revisits Fred Brooks's The Mythical Man-Month to ask whether its lessons hold when a single developer orchestrates a swarm of AI coding agents. He argues the human expert still feels essential, bringing rigor and continuous supervision through tools like roborev rather than "prompt and chill" vibe coding. The open question is whether parallel agents escape the old bottlenecks of scope creep, architectural drift, and coordination overhead, or simply reintroduce them at machine speed.

title: When I reject AI code even if it works
blurb: The author argues that as AI accelerates implementation, the real bottleneck shifts to reviewing code the developer never reasoned through, producing cognitive overload that makes him reject working changes and start over. He rejects AI code when he can't explain the approach in his own words, when the diff exceeds the problem, or when it adds unproven abstractions, advocating mandatory human review alongside AI checks.

title: SpaceX to buy Cursor AI coding agent operator Anysphere for $60B
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire Anysphere, the company behind the Cursor AI coding agent, for $60B. The deal would fold one of the most widely used AI coding tools into Elon Musk's space and satellite empire, signaling SpaceX's push to build out internal AI software capabilities and developer infrastructure.

title: Stripe and AWS enable AI agent payments for content owners and publishers
blurb: Stripe and AWS introduced infrastructure that lets AI agents make payments on behalf of users while compensating the content owners and publishers whose material the agents access. The integration combines Stripe's payment rails with AWS Bedrock and AgentCore, giving publishers a way to meter and monetize agent traffic rather than have their content scraped for free.

title: Report: GKE Inference Gateway delivers up to 92% faster AI responses
blurb: Google's GKE Inference Gateway uses prefix caching and model-aware routing to direct LLM requests to accelerators already holding the relevant KV cache in memory, skipping recomputation of repeated prompt prefixes. An independent benchmark found it delivered 15.7% higher throughput, 92.8% shorter wait times, and 62.6% lower inter-token latency than the next leading managed Kubernetes service, with Snap reporting prefix cache hit rates of 75-80%.

title: WeChat begins testing Xiaowei as Tencent eyes a Q3 AI rollout
blurb: Tencent has begun testing Xiaowei, an AI assistant embedded directly in WeChat, with a small group of users ahead of a planned public rollout in Q3. Rather than a standalone chatbot, Xiaowei acts as a command layer across the app's mini-programs, drawing mainly on Weixin's own LLM and turning to DeepSeek for some queries, with the longer aim of handling payments and services on spoken or typed instruction.

title: The minimum viable unit of saleable software
blurb: LLMs flipped the buy-vs-build calculus, so the cheap internal package that replaces your $400/mo SaaS is now a real threat to anyone selling software. But cheap isn't zero, and the author's point is that LLM-built systems still demand dozens of feedback loops to get right plus an open-ended maintenance bill for every new feature and bug. The opening for saleable software is the work LLMs make easier but not free, where the ongoing cost of owning the thing yourself still outweighs the price of buying it.

title: Sources: Shanghai-based Coowa, which develops embodied AI robots, plans to file a Hong Kong IPO; Coowa was valued at $3B after raising $600M in its latest round (Jiahui Huang/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Coowa, a Shanghai-based maker of embodied AI robots, plans to file for a Hong Kong IPO after a $600M round valued it at $3B. The move signals that embodied AI is graduating from lab demos to public-market scale, with Chinese hardware startups leading the rush. For founders building physical AI, the capital and exit path are concentrating fast in Asia, where manufacturing depth and patient state-linked funding give robotics companies a runway their Western counterparts still have to assemble piece by piece.

title: SoftBank-Backed Robot Maker Gears Up for Hong Kong IPO, Sources Say
blurb: Coowa, the SoftBank-backed maker of robots, is preparing a Hong Kong IPO after raising more than $600 million in a round that valued it above $3 billion. The listing pulls a humanoid and service-robotics player into public markets while most of the field is still private and burning cash on hardware. For founders, it sets an early valuation benchmark for the category and signals that public-market appetite for embodied AI is real enough to test, not just venture-stage enthusiasm.

title: The PIK Fuse: How Private Equity Software Deals Like Medallia Actually Blow Up, and Exactly Who’s Next
blurb: Private equity loaded a dozen software leaders with debt at the 2021 peak, and the PIK toggle is what hides the damage until it's terminal. Payment-in-kind lets a borrower defer cash interest and roll it onto the principal, so the balance compounds while the business weakens and covenant tests still pass right up until the deferral window closes. Thoma Bravo just handed Medallia to its lenders, wiping out $5.1B in equity on a company earning $200M a year against $300M in debt service. It is the second giant wipeout in 18 months, and the fuse runs the same way every time.

title: Revolut and Synthesia early backer Seedcamp raises $320M, invests in US
blurb: Seedcamp, the early backer of Revolut and Synthesia, raised $320M and is opening a dedicated New York office to build a "Transatlantic Bridge" for the European startups it funds. The capital splits into a $220M seventh first-check fund and a $100M growth fund aimed at Series B and beyond, with the US presence meant to plug founders into American capital, hires, and customers from day one. The bet is that Europe's best companies no longer wait for permission to go global, so their lead investor is meeting them on the ground in the US.

title: GEO: How to Get Your Content Cited by AI Search Engines (With Data from the Princeton Study)
blurb: AI search engines no longer hand out blue links, they generate answers and cite a handful of sources, so if ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews never quote you, you are invisible in the fastest-growing search channel. The Princeton GEO study tested nine optimization tactics across 10,000 queries and found the ones that win are statistics with sources, expert quotes, Schema.org markup, and clean extractable structure, while old-school keyword stuffing actively hurts. The bigger opening is that lower-ranked pages benefit most, with a position-five page gaining 115% visibility while position one lost 30%, so small publishers finally have room SEO never gave them.

title: Revolut, Wise and UiPath-backer Seedcamp raises $320m
blurb: Seedcamp, the European early-stage firm behind Revolut, Wise, and UiPath, raised $320m across two new funds to keep writing first checks into the region's startups. The firm is leaning into the sovereignty wave sweeping European tech, but drawing a hard line: it won't back companies whose only differentiator is being local. The signal for founders is that capital is flowing again to European pre-seed, but the bar is the same as anywhere, and "we're sovereign" is a feature, not a business.

title: The minimum viable unit of saleable software
blurb: The author argues that LLMs have shifted the buy-vs-build calculus but not eliminated the case for selling software, since LLM-built systems still demand dozens of feedback loops to reach quality and carry ongoing maintenance cost. His bet on turning the River side project into a business rests on cheap not meaning free, leaving room for polished, well-maintained packages that internal LLM clones rarely match.

title: A 3D voxel game engine written in APL
blurb: A developer built an experimental 3D voxel game engine in APL, using the notation to drive a Vulkan, DirectX12, and Metal rendering backend.

title: Apple’s New CEO Must Rebuild a Design Team That Lost Its Way
blurb: Bloomberg reports that incoming Apple CEO John Ternus inherits a design organization that has drifted since Jony Ive's departure, with no clear successor to set hardware and interface direction. The same briefing outlines Apple's 2027 roadmap, including a redesigned iPhone and updated AirPods, raising the stakes for whoever Ternus installs to lead design.

title: Clap Studio creates "the most music-respectful room" in Spook nightclub
blurb: Clap Studio renovated the El Cubo room at Valencia's Spook nightclub by borrowing acoustic principles from "dead rooms," using sound-absorbing surfaces and minimal reflective materials to prioritize music clarity over visual spectacle. The project, a follow-up to the studio's work on sister club Oven, treats acoustics as the primary design constraint rather than an afterthought layered onto a finished interior.

title: AMD will reinstate memory encryption on Ryzen 9000 CPUs via BIOS update in July
blurb: AMD will reinstate Transparent Secure Memory Encryption (TSME) on non-PRO Ryzen 9000 desktop CPUs via a July BIOS update, reversing a quiet removal in AGESA 1.2.7.0 that disabled the firmware-level RAM encryption present since 2020. TSME defends against cold-boot attacks where an attacker with physical access extracts sensitive data from memory, so affected fleets should apply the July microcode and confirm encryption status post-update.

title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
blurb: AryStinger malware has infected at least 4,300 legacy routers, exploiting CVE-2013-3307 in Linksys and CVE-2016-5681 in D-Link models to build a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network rather than a DDoS botnet. QiAnXin's XLab reports the pool is mostly D-Link DIR-850L devices, skewing to South Korea and China, with a second Go-based strain hitting QNAP NAS boxes via the patched CVE-2025-11837.

title: AMD will reinstate memory encryption on Ryzen 9000 CPUs via BIOS update in July
blurb: AMD will reinstate Transparent Secure Memory Encryption (TSME) on consumer Ryzen 9000 desktop CPUs through a July BIOS update, reversing a quiet removal it shipped in the AGESA 1.2.7.0 firmware earlier this year. The RAM-encryption feature, branded Memory Guard on Ryzen PRO parts, guards against cold-boot physical-access attacks, and its return after community pushback narrows the security gap AMD had opened between its consumer and PRO lineups.

title: The systemd 261 release brings a software TPM, new OS installer
blurb: systemd 261 shipped with an IMDS subsystem that exposes cloud instance metadata through a local Varlink API, recognizing providers including AWS EC2, Azure, Google Compute Engine, and Oracle Cloud, plus a software TPM service built on IBM's swtpm for hardware that lacks a physical one. The release also adds state persistence across kexec reboots, a textual OS installer in systemd-sysinstall, and graduates systemd-sysupdate out of experimental status.

title: Anthropic's Mythos mess just keeps getting more complicated
blurb: Anthropic disabled its Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models for all customers after the Trump administration issued an export-control order last week barring any foreign national, including Anthropic staff, from using them on national-security grounds. The Register's Kettle podcast digs into Amazon's possible role in the decision and warns that open-weight models will likely match the same capabilities, making the restriction hard to enforce for enterprise buyers caught without access.

title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
blurb: AryStinger malware has infected at least 4,300 legacy routers, mostly D-Link DIR-850L units running Realtek RTL819X chips, turning them into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network that scans, fingerprints, and tunnels traffic before an attacker breaks in. A second strain hits QNAP NAS boxes through CVE-2025-11837, a flaw patched in November 2025, so unpatched appliances and end-of-life routers remain the exposed surface IT teams need to retire or segment.

title: The systemd 261 release brings a software TPM, new OS installer
blurb: systemd 261 shipped with an IMDS subsystem that exposes a local Varlink API for cloud instance metadata, recognizing EC2, Azure, GCE, Hetzner, and Oracle Cloud by their SMBIOS data. The release also adds kexec state preservation via FileDescriptorStorePreserve, a software TPM service backed by IBM's swtpm, and systemd-sysinstall, a Varlink-based textual OS installer.

title: LinkedIn lets users set core brand rules
blurb: LinkedIn is rolling out brand kits in Campaign Manager, letting marketers set color palettes, fonts, and a brand voice that its AI tools then apply to ad and content generation. LinkedIn auto-assembles a starting brand voice from a company's existing page and past posts, aiming to cut off-brand mistakes and manual rework in AI-drafted ads.

title: Snapchat launches AI-powered ad creation tools
blurb: Snapchat released a suite of AI-powered ad creation tools, including a chatbot-guided campaign setup and the Snap Creator Network that matches advertisers with relevant creator partners. The setup assistant lowers the barrier for smaller advertisers running Snap campaigns, while the creator-matching layer is Snap's bid to capture more influencer spend that currently flows to TikTok and Instagram.

title: Snapchat launches ‘House of the Dragon’ AR ad in New York City
blurb: Snapchat launched an interactive AR ad for HBO's "House of the Dragon" in Times Square, letting people project themselves onto large-format billboards and insert themselves into scenes from the show. The activation pairs Snapchat's AR tech with high-traffic out-of-home placement, giving brands a templated way to turn static billboards into shareable, camera-driven moments that extend reach into social.

title: Google adds AI bot to Ad Manager
blurb: Google added a Gemini-powered AI assistant to Ad Manager that answers campaign questions in-stream, giving advertisers personalized guidance without leaving the dashboard. The move pushes routine campaign troubleshooting and optimization queries toward an AI layer, which could compress the time teams spend digging through Google Ads documentation and support to diagnose underperforming campaigns.

title: GEO: How to Get Your Content Cited by AI Search Engines (With Data from the Princeton Study)
blurb: The Princeton GEO study (KDD 2024) tested 9 content-optimization strategies across 10,000 queries and found that adding cited statistics, expert quotations, and clean extractable structure raises citation rates in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews, while keyword stuffing actively hurts. The bigger signal for smaller publishers is the democratization effect: a page ranked fifth gained 115% more visibility in AI answers, while the top-ranked page lost 30%. The practical checklist is to allow AI crawlers in robots.txt, back claims with sourced data, and add Schema.org markup.

title: The PIK Fuse: How Private Equity Software Deals Like Medallia Actually Blow Up, and Exactly Who’s Next
blurb: Thoma Bravo handed Medallia to its lenders in April, wiping out roughly $5.1B in equity after buying the company for $6.4B in 2021. The trigger was a PIK toggle that let debt service compound to ~$300M a year against ~$200M in earnings, hiding the gap until the deferral window closed. The author argues a dozen more peak-era PE software deals are on the same fuse, with timing knowable because the pattern repeats every time.
